perturbation.original_text: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.changed_text: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause c. from Section 5 (Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers), it creates an in-text contradiction. Section 5 is about preventing the employee from taking actions that could harm the Banking Organization's relationship with its customers. By removing clause c, the agreement now only prohibits soliciting or persuading customers to change their relationship with the Financial Institution, but it does not prevent the employee from directly providing Banking and Financial Services or directly accepting Banking and Financial Services from any Customer or Potential Customer. This contradicts the overall intent of the section, because it does not prohibit the employee from engaging directly with customers to provide services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits clause (c) from Section 5, which originally prohibited employees from directly providing or accepting Banking and Financial Services from customers. The explanation states that removing this clause creates a contradiction because Section 5 is intended to prevent actions that could harm the Banking Organization's customer relationships. By omitting the clause, the agreement no longer explicitly prohibits the employee from directly engaging with customers to provide services, which contradicts the overall intent of the section described in the Contradicted Text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is introduced.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9 states that a breach of Sections 4, 5, or 6 will cause irreparable damage. Removing Section 5 creates an in-text contradiction, which affects the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement. Because Section 5 is omitted, but it still exists, if its breached there is no clause to enforce it, creating uncertainty. Section 9 still claims that violations of those sections will cause irreparable damage and injury, but the omission makes it more difficult to prove that violations of section 5 lead to such damage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that breaches of Sections 4, 5, or 6 will cause irreparable damage and injury. The changed text removes Section 5 from that list. However, Section 5 still exists in the contract (as shown in the contradicted text). This creates a contradiction because the contract no longer explicitly states that a breach of Section 5 will cause irreparable damage, even though Section 5 is a non-solicitation agreement (Customers) that the original text would have protected. The omission in the changed text weakens the enforceability of Section 5, directly contradicting the implicit understanding that all three sections (4, 5, and 6) were equally protected and would cause irreparable harm if breached.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6 , are reasonable and necessary to protect a Banking Organization. Employee agrees that the covenants, restrictions and obligations will not affect Employee’s ability to make a living.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Section 6 , are reasonable and necessary to protect a Banking Organization. Employee agrees that the covenants, restrictions and obligations will not affect Employee’s ability to make a living.
perturbation.explanation: Section 19 acknowledges that the restrictions in Sections 5 and 6 are reasonable. By removing the reference to Section 5, it creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in its enforceability. Now section 19 only acknowledge Section 6 but there is nothing being acknowledged about section 5. This section is no longer enforceable. There's no longer any legal or factual basis to claim that section 5 is reasonable and necessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledges the reasonableness of restrictions in Sections 5 and 6. The changed text removes the reference to Section 5, acknowledging only Section 6. The contradicted text is Section 5, which outlines non-solicitation restrictions. By removing the acknowledgment of reasonableness for Section 5, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction. Specifically, the enforceability of Section 5 is now uncertain because there is no longer any expressed legal basis to claim that Section 5 is reasonable and necessary, as Section 19 only acknowledges Section 6. This contradicts the existence and enforceability of Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization is entitled to the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 5 and 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will not begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization is entitled to the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will not begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 6 .
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 talks about 'Tolling of Covenants', which means the time during which the non-compete and non-solicitation agreements are effective can be extended if the employee violates the agreement. The original section mentions both sections 5 and 6, but now we only mention section 6. This leads to the question of how to handle breaches of section 5. By omitting section 5 here, it creates uncertainty about the consequences of breaching Section 5. If there are any violations, section 7 won't be enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 7 referred to both Sections 5 and 6 regarding the tolling of covenants. The changed text omits Section 5, meaning a breach of Section 5 may not trigger the tolling provision as intended. This directly contradicts Section 5, which defines the non-solicitation obligations of the employee and expects that breaches will result in irreparable harm, financial loss, lost goodwill and Confidential Information. By removing the reference to Section 5 in the tolling provision, the contract creates uncertainty and inconsistency regarding the consequences of breaching Section 5, making it potentially unenforceable and therefore a strong in-text contradiction.
